title: dryopteris urbani
side_effects: Dryopteris urbani can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Prolonged consumption may lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its potent saponins. In rare cases, excessive exposure to the plant's toxic compounds may cause kidney damage, liver inflammation, and even neurological symptoms like tremors and seizures. If not handled carefully, the plant's volatile oils can also trigger anaphylaxis in sensitive individuals.
